title: Salted Caramel Cheesecake
excerpt: This is not a cheap cheesecake, but you really do cut small slices because it's so rich. Make it at least 24 hours ahead of time. I made mine 48 hours ahead. Take the cream cheese out of the fridge beforehand so that it's nice and soft. I tip my …
html: This is not a cheap cheesecake, but you really do cut small slices because it's so rich. Make it at least 24 hours ahead of time. I made mine 48 hours ahead. Take the cream cheese out of the fridge beforehand so that it's nice and soft. I tip my caramel out of the tin and stir it first with a spoon so that it's soft.

ingredientGroups.title: Crust
items: 1 packet Nuttikrust biscuits finely crushed in the food processor (you can use any biscuit of your choice)
75g salted pretzels also finely crushed in the food processor

ingredientGroups.title: Cheesecake filling
items: 10 g gelatine (one sachet) dissolved in 90 ml water (I stir mine with warm water and then quickly put it in the microwave until it's dissolved. Literally a few seconds)

directionGroups.title: Crust
steps: Grease a springform pan. I lined the bottom of my springform pan with baking paper that hangs well over the edges. This just makes it easier to get the cheesecake out.
Mix all the ingredients for the crust together and press it firmly into the springform pan. Put it in the fridge while you make the cheesecake.

directionGroups.title: Cheesecake filling
steps: Using an electric beater, beat the cream cheese, vanilla and icing sugar together until smooth.
Beat in the cream, gelatine and salt. Beat for a little while until it thickens.
Pour into your springform pan and let it set in the fridge for at least 24 hours.

directionGroups.title: Decorate
steps: Here you can do anything you like. I piped extra caramel treat on top and then added caramel popcorn and more pretzels.
